title: Bitfarms Advances U.S. Strategy with Completion of Stronghold Digital Mining Acquisition
text: Bitfarms finalized acquisition of Stronghold Digital Mining through stock-for-stock merger on March 17, 2025. Stronghold shareholders received 2.52 Bitfarms shares per share held, with approximately 59.7 million common shares and 10.6 million warrants issued. Company paid $44.5 million to retire Stronghold's outstanding debt. Combined entity manages 623 megawatts including 165 MW of active generating capacity and 142 MW of immediately available grid import capacity. Acquisition secures 1.1 GW growth pipeline across three Pennsylvania sites within PJM market. Deal establishes Bitfarms as leading Bitcoin miner in PJM region. Year-end 2025 projections show energy portfolio shifting to 80% North American and 20% international operations. Acquisition enables development of two power campuses totaling nearly 1 GW dedicated to high-performance computing and artificial intelligence with strategic partners WWT and ASG prioritizing Stronghold sites for conversion. Bitfarms gains nearly 1 exahash through existing Canaan hosting agreements, bringing total management capacity to 18 exahash.
